title: orthocarpus pusillus var. typicus
intro:
Orthocarpus pusillus var. typicus, also known as the small bird's beak or tiny bird's beak, is a perennial plant native to western North America, characterized by its small, yellow or orange flowers and distinctive beak-shaped leaves.

This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Orthocarpus pusillus var. typicus include its ability to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues, colds, and flu, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve respiratory health. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
